title: Process for the catalytic oxidation of ammonia to nitric oxide
abstract: Process for the catalytic oxidation of ammonia to nitric oxide in two oxidation steps in presence of a catalyst comprising one or more transition metal oxides.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. Process for the catalytic oxidation of ammonia to ni tric oxide comprising the steps of (a) providing a first gas stream comprising ammonia and ox ygen; (b) providing a second gas stream comprising ammonia; (c) oxidizing the ammonia contained in the first gas stream with oxygen contained in the gas stream by contact with a catalyst comprising one or more transition metal oxides in a first oxidation step; (d) cooling the oxidized first gas stream from step (c)to obtain a first effluent stream comprising an amount of ni tric oxide; (e) admixing the first effluent stream withdrawn from step (d)with the second gas stream; (f) contacting the mixed first effluent stream from step (e) with a catalyst comprising one or more transition metal oxides and oxidizing the ammonia contained in the second gas stream in a second oxidation step; (g) withdrawing a second effluent stream from step (f)and cooling the second effluent stream to obtain a product stream comprising nitric oxide in amount higher than the amount in the first effluent stream. 2. Process of claim 1, wherein the second gas stream fur ther comprises oxygen. 3. Process of claim 1 or 2, wherein the second gas stream is obtained from a split stream of the first gas stream prior to step (c). 4. Process of any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein the first gas stream is prepared by admixing ammonia into atmospheric air. 5. Process of any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the first gas stream is prepared by admixing ammonia into oxygen en riched air. 6. Process of any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein addi tional amounts of oxygen are added to the mixed first ef fluent stream and/or to the first gas stream. 7. Process of any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the oxi dized first gas stream is cooled in step (d) to a tempera ture of between 200 and 500Â°C. 8. Process of any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein content of the ammonia in the first gas stream is adjusted to re sult in an oxidation temperature in step (c) of between 650 and 850Â°C, preferably between 700 and 800Â°C. 9. Process of any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the amount of the ammonia in the second gas stream admixed in the first effluent in step (e) is adjusted to result in an 

oxidation temperature in step (f) of between 650 and 850Â°C preferably between 700 and 800Â°C. 10. Process of any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein the inlet temperature to the first oxidation step is between ambient temperature and 250Â°C, preferably between 100Â°C and 200Â°C. 11. Process of any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the cooling of the oxidized first stream is performed by cool- ing means comprising a steam superheater. 12. Process of any one of claims 1 to 11, wherein the cooling of the second effluent stream is performed in a boiler. 13. Process of claim 12, wherein steam produced in the boiler is used in the steam superheater. 14. Process of any one of claims 1 to 13, wherein the transition metal oxides of the catalyst comprise at least one of Mn, Fe, Co, Ni, Cu, Zn, A1. 15. Process of claim 14, wherein the transition metal ox ides are promoted with Ce and/or La. 16. Process of any one of claims 1 to 15, wherein the cat alyst is arranged in fixed bed manner. 17. Process of any one of claims 1 to 16, wherein the sec- ond effluent is used in the preparation of nitric acid. 18. Method for revamping an existing process for the cata lytic oxidation of ammonia to nitric oxide, the existing process comprises the steps of (a) providing a gas stream comprising ammonia and oxygen; (b) oxidizing the ammonia contained in the gas stream with oxygen contained in the gas stream by contact with a PGM group catalyst; (c) cooling the oxidized gas stream to obtain an effluent stream comprising an amount of nitric oxide; the method comprising the steps of (i) providing a second gas stream comprising ammonia; (ii) replacing the PGM catalyst in step (b)with a catalyst comprising one or more transition metal oxides; (iii) admixing the effluent stream withdrawn from step (c) with of the second gas stream; (iv) contacting the mixed effluent stream from step (iv) with a catalyst comprising one or more transition metal ox ides and oxidizing the ammonia contained in the second gas stream; (v) cooling the oxidized gas stream from step (iv); and (vi) withdrawing an effluent stream from step (v)to obtain a product stream comprising nitric oxide.
